title: Parallel Agent Support (Session Multiplexing + Multi-Process)
overview: Add session multiplexing to @ulpi/browse so multiple AI agents can share a single server with isolated browser contexts. Each session gets its own BrowserManager (tabs, refs, cookies, storage, buffers) while sharing one Chromium process. Multi-process isolation via BROWSE_PORT continues to work as a fallback.
scopeChallenge: BrowserManager is already self-contained per instance — no state leaks. The blockers are: module-level buffer singletons in buffers.ts, single BrowserManager in server.ts, no session concept in HTTP protocol, and global idle timer. Session multiplexing is the primary approach (efficient, no port coordination); multi-process via BROWSE_PORT is the fallback (already works, just needs wider range).

failureModes.mitigation: Document that crash recovery restarts ALL sessions. Agents must re-navigate.
closeIdleSessions GC + server shutdown when all idle. Log session lifecycle.
Keep defaultBuffers export for backward compat.

failureModes.risk: Race condition: two agents create same session ID
failureModes.mitigation: getOrCreate is synchronous map lookup — no race.

failureModes.risk: Session header not forwarded on retry
failureModes.mitigation: sendCommand captures sessionId in closure — retries inherit it.
Move flush cursors into SessionBuffers class (TASK-001).

testCoverageMap.codepath: --session CLI flag parsing and header injection
Per-session buffer read in console/network commands
Two concurrent CLI processes with independent state

tasks.title: Refactor Buffers Into Per-Session Class
tasks.description: Move console/network buffers from module-level singletons into a SessionBuffers class. Update BrowserManager to accept a SessionBuffers instance instead of importing globals. Keep defaultBuffers export for backward compatibility.
acceptanceCriteria: SessionBuffers class holds its own console/network arrays and counters
BrowserManager constructor accepts optional SessionBuffers, defaults to defaultBuffers
All existing tests pass without modification (backward compat via defaults)
Creating two SessionBuffers instances produces independent buffer state

tasks.title: Create SessionManager
tasks.description: Create SessionManager class that manages Map<string, Session>. Each session has its own BrowserManager, SessionBuffers, and idle timestamp. Sessions share a single Chromium Browser instance. Methods: getOrCreate, closeSession, closeIdleSessions, listSessions, closeAll, getAllSessions, getSessionCount.
acceptanceCriteria: getOrCreate returns existing session or creates new one with isolated BrowserManager + buffers
closeSession closes the browser context and removes from map
closeIdleSessions returns list of closed session IDs and frees their resources
Creating two sessions and navigating to different URLs produces independent state

tasks.title: Split BrowserManager.launch Into Shared Browser Mode
tasks.description: Add launchWithBrowser(browser) method to BrowserManager that creates a context on an existing Browser (shared Chromium). Update close() to only close context (not browser) when in shared mode. Add ownsTheBrowser flag.
acceptanceCriteria: launchWithBrowser(browser) creates a new context without launching Chromium
close() in shared mode closes only the context, not the browser
close() in owned mode (via launch()) still closes the browser entirely
Two BrowserManager instances sharing the same Browser have independent contexts

tasks.title: Wire Session Header Into Server HTTP Handler
tasks.description: Update server.ts to extract X-Browse-Session header from requests, resolve session via SessionManager, and pass session's BrowserManager + SessionBuffers to command handlers. Replace single browserManager with SessionManager. Update handleCommand, start(), shutdown(), and /health.
acceptanceCriteria: Requests without X-Browse-Session header use session ID 'default' (backward compatible)
Requests with different session IDs get independent BrowserManagers
/health response includes sessions count
Shutdown closes all sessions cleanly

tasks.title: Update Read Commands for Per-Session Buffers
tasks.description: Update commands/read.ts to accept optional SessionBuffers parameter. The console and network commands must read from the session's own buffers instead of global singletons. Falls back to global buffers when not provided (backward compat for tests).
acceptanceCriteria: console command reads from session-specific buffer when SessionBuffers is provided
network command reads from session-specific buffer when SessionBuffers is provided
Without SessionBuffers argument, falls back to global buffers (existing tests pass)
Two sessions produce independent console/network output

tasks.title: Add --session Flag to CLI
tasks.description: Add --session <id> flag to cli.ts that sets X-Browse-Session header on all HTTP requests. Also support BROWSE_SESSION env var. Update help text.
acceptanceCriteria: browse --session agent-1 goto url sends X-Browse-Session: agent-1 header
BROWSE_SESSION=agent-1 browse goto ... works equivalently
Without --session, no header is sent (server uses default session)
--session flag is consumed and not passed as a command argument

tasks.title: Per-Session Buffer Flush to Disk
tasks.description: Update server.ts buffer flush logic to iterate all sessions and flush each session's buffers to separate log files (browse-console-<session>.log). Move flush cursors into SessionBuffers. Add getAllSessions() to SessionManager.
acceptanceCriteria: Each session's logs flush to separate files named by session ID
Default session uses existing file names (backward compatible)
Shutdown flushes all sessions' buffers
Flush cursors are per-session

tasks.title: Per-Session Idle Cleanup
tasks.description: Replace global idle timer with per-session idle tracking. Individual sessions are GC'd after their own idle timeout. Server shuts down when all sessions are idle and removed.
acceptanceCriteria: Individual sessions close after idle timeout without affecting other active sessions
Server shuts down when ALL sessions have been idle-closed
Active sessions prevent server shutdown
Session that receives a new command is NOT closed (activity resets)

tasks.title: Session Management Commands
tasks.description: Add 'sessions' (list all), 'session-close <id>' commands to meta.ts. Update status to show session info. Register in META_COMMANDS set in server.ts.
acceptanceCriteria: browse sessions lists all active sessions with tab count, URL, idle time
browse session-close <id> closes the specified session
browse session-close on non-existent session returns clear error
browse status includes session count

tasks.title: Widen Port Range for Multi-Process Mode
tasks.description: Change PORT_RANGE_END from 9410 to 9450 in constants.ts, allowing 51 concurrent server instances for environments preferring full process isolation.
acceptanceCriteria: PORT_RANGE_END is 9450
Port scanning still stops at first available port
BROWSE_PORT and BROWSE_PORT_START overrides still work

tasks.title: Integration Tests for Session Multiplexing
tasks.description: Write integration tests verifying two sessions are fully isolated: independent navigation, refs, buffers, tab state. Test session lifecycle (create, close, idle cleanup).
acceptanceCriteria: Tests verify navigation isolation (different URLs per session)
Tests verify buffer isolation (console/network entries don't leak)
Tests verify ref isolation (snapshot refs from session A can't be used in session B)
Tests verify session lifecycle (close one, other survives)

tasks.title: Update README and CLAUDE.md for Sessions
tasks.description: Document --session flag, BROWSE_SESSION env var, session management commands, and parallel agent usage patterns in README. Update CLAUDE.md architecture summary and command categories.
acceptanceCriteria: README has a Parallel Agents section showing multi-agent usage with --session
Environment variables table includes BROWSE_SESSION
Command reference includes sessions and session-close
CLAUDE.md reflects new session architecture

tasks.title: End-to-End CLI Session Test
tasks.description: Test full CLI flow: two concurrent browse --session invocations hitting the same server with independent state. Spawn CLI processes, verify independent responses.
acceptanceCriteria: Two concurrent CLI processes with different --session flags get independent browser state
Session A navigation does not affect Session B page content
CLI without --session uses default session (backward compatible)
Server state file is shared (single server serves both sessions)
